channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Some artists lost their life’s work in LA wildfires. Fellow artists are helping them recover
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Anthony Obi never imagined the night of Jan. 7 would be the last time he’d step inside his safe haven.
item.dc:creator: LESLIE AMBRIZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Broncos to supply helmets to every Colorado high school tackle football team
item.description: The Broncos have partnered with a football equipment company and the Colorado High School Activities Association to supply more than 15,000 brand-new Riddell Axiom helmets.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, NewsNation

item.title: Trump rescinds funding freeze but uncertainty lives on for wide array of environmental programs
item.description: A chaotic back-and-forth over President Donald Trump’s freeze on federal grants and loans has farmers, activists, organizations and businesses wondering whether they will receive the funding they rely on.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man who killed five people after a neighbor complained about his gunfire gets life in prison
item.description: COLDSPRING, Texas (AP) — A man accused of killing five people in Texas in 2023 after a neighbor complained that he was keeping a baby awake by firing a gun in his yard late at night pleaded guilty Wednesday to murder charges and will serve a life sentence without the possibility of parole, a prosecu
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amber Alert issued for sisters allegedly taken by father suspected of killing their mother
item.description: Authorities in Central California have issued an Amber Alert for two missing sisters whose mother was found dead at their home, allegedly killed by their father.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kansas unveils a mural honoring 'rebel women' who campaigned for voting rights
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Kansas has a new mural in its Statehouse honoring women who campaigned for voting rights for decades before the 1920 ratification of the 19th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution granted those rights across the nation. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: CVS gives some customers a way to get into locked cabinets
item.description: Instead of waiting for an employee to take pity on you and unlock the cabinets in the deodorant aisle, you may soon have another way to access the locked-up shampoo and ibuprofen you seek.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Things to know about the Trump administration order on miles per gallon for cars and pickups
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Hours after being sworn in as the new U.S. Secretary of Transportation, Sean Duffy took aim at the main way the federal government regulates miles per gallon for cars and pickup trucks — also a principal way that it regulates air pollution and addresses climate change.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hawaii wildfire victims spared from testifying after last-minute deal over $4B settlement
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Lawyers representing victims of a deadly Hawaii wildfire reached a last-minute deal averting a trial Wednesday to determine how to split a $4 billion settlement.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs Laken Riley Act, marking first legislative win of second term
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed the Laken Riley Act into law, marking his first legislative victory since he returned to the White House after he campaigned on immigration and the border during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Gymnast dies after falling from pole while celebrating Philadelphia Eagles win
item.description: Tyler Sabapathy, 18, "sustained critical injuries" in the fall and passed away Tuesday, Temple University announced.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, Fox 5

item.title: Fed holds rates steady as Trump tariffs loom, economy hums
item.description: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady on Wednesday after price increases ticked up over the last three months, suggesting inflation may still have some life in it.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Cold-stunned green sea turtles are recovering at a Florida marine life center
item.description: JUNO BEACH, Fla. (AP) — An arctic blast that brought a rare snowfall to northern Florida last week left green sea turtles as far south as St. Augustine suffering from a condition known as cold stunning.
item.dc:creator: CODY JACKSON and FREIDA FRISARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump White House rescinds memo freezing federal money after widespread confusion
item.description: President Donald Trump’s budget office has rescinded a memo freezing spending on federal grants, less than two days after it sparked widespread confusion and legal challenges across the country.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Republican congressman suggests students get jobs to pay for lunches
item.description: Republican Rep. Ritch McCormick (R-Ga.) suggested that students should get jobs to pay for their lunches as the federal funding freeze proposed by President Trump threatens nutrition support for children across the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Scottie Scheffler details his hand injury from making ravioli and is ready to get back to golf
item.description: PEBBLE BEACH, Calif. (AP) — Scottie Scheffler felt frustrated sitting at home for two tournaments he normally plays while recovering from glass puncturing the palm of his right hand while making ravioli. A big part of him was thankful the injury wasn't worse.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump fires two Democratic commissioners of agency that enforces civil rights laws in the workplace
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — President Donald Trump fired two of the three Democratic commissioners of the federal agency that enforces civil rights law in the workplace, an unprecedented move aimed at implementing his crackdown on certain diversity and gender rights policies.
item.dc:creator: ALEXANDRA OLSON and CLAIRE SAVAGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida Republican introduces bill to add Trump to Mount Rushmore
item.description: The Interior Department would be in charge of undertaking Trump’s addition to the South Dakota landmark if approved by Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Senate Judiciary advances Bondi AG nomination on party-line vote
item.description: President Trump’s pick to lead the Department of Justice, Pam Bondi, on Wednesday advanced out of the Senate Judiciary Committee on a strict party-line vote, setting the stage for a vote on the Senate floor.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Video: Man dragged out of Tennessee school board meeting
item.description: "Do you think the community is blind to what's going on? Do you think we are stupid," one attendee said.
item.dc:creator: Brian Didlake, NewsNation

item.title: 2 West Haven police officers shot while executing search warrant in New Haven, suspect dead
item.description: Two West Haven police officers while executing a search warrant early Wednesday morning at an apartment complex in New Haven, and the suspect was fatally shot, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, NewsNation

item.title: Honda recalls more than 290,000 vehicles over possible engine issue
item.description: An error in the fuel injection electronic control unit could lead to the engine stalling or a loss os power.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: Harris team planned Houston rally with Joe Rogan in mind
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris made a substantial effort to get on Joe Rogan’s popular podcast amid concerns about her traction with male voters — going so far as to plan a Houston rally to justify a stop at Rogan’s Austin studio, according to a forthcoming book.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Frontier makes a second bid for Spirit with budget airlines struggling
item.description: Frontier Airlines is attempting for a second time to merge with the now bankrupt Spirit Airlines, which declared bankruptcy late last year as budget airlines struggle.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump makes moves to expand power, sparking chaos, possible constitutional crisis
item.description: Just a little over a week into his second term, President Donald Trump is taking steps to maximize his power, sparking chaos and what critics contend is a constitutional crisis as he challenges the separation of powers that have defined American government for more than 200 years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The Year of the Snake is underway with Lunar New Year festivities in Asia and around the world
item.description: Lunar New Year festivals and prayers marked the start of the Year of the Snake around Asia and farther afield on Wednesday — including in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DC Council proposes bill to ban cell phones in district's public schools
item.description: The Washington, D.C., Council is considering a total cellphone ban in the district's public schools to limit distractions in the classroom and force students to socialize.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deeply disturbing statistics show Texas children under the age of 11 left state for abortion in 2023
item.description: Newly released statistics from 2023 show that minors in Texas are traveling out of the state to have abortions and the numbers are rising.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: While signing Laken Riley Act, Trump says he'll send 'worst criminal aliens' to Guantanamo
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday used a signing ceremony for the Laken Riley Act to announce that his administration plans to send the “worst criminal aliens” to detention in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former US Sen. Bob Menendez gets 11 years in prison for taking bribes and acting as agent of Egypt
item.description: Former U_S_ Sen_ Bob Menendez has been sentenced to 11 years in prison for his conviction for accepting bribes of gold and cash and acting as an agent of Egypt, crimes his own lawyer said earned him the nickname "Gold Bar Bob.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Alyssa Thomas heading to Mercury from Sun in trade, AP source says
item.description: The Phoenix Mercury were finalizing a deal to acquire Alyssa Thomas from the Connecticut Sun, a person familiar with the trade told The Associated Press on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Public confidence in US health agencies declines, survey says
item.description: Trust in health institutions tended to fall along party lines, with Democrats showing greater support.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: US education agency investigating Denver schools over all-gender bathroom
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The U.S. Education Department said Tuesday it was investigating Denver Public Schools for alleged discrimination after the district converted a girl’s restroom on the second floor of a high school into an all-gender restroom while leaving another bathroom exclusive to boys.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC home to hundreds of thousands of undocumented people: Reports
item.description: The Trump administration is pushing to increase the arrests of undocumented people, issuing daily quotas for the tri-state area, according to The Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars. U.S. District Judge Loren L. AliKhan blocked the action Tuesday afternoon, minutes before it was set to go into effect.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: States lose access to Medicaid payment portal amid Trump federal aid freeze
item.description: State Medicaid programs across the country are reporting they've lost access to federal payment portals one day after President Trump announced a freeze on federal grants and aid.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American Airlines worker struck, killed by vehicle at Charlotte Airport
item.description: A worker at Charlotte Douglas International Airport (CLT) died Monday after being struck by an airline ramp vehicle on the tarmac, local police have confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, WGN 9
